description_long: This object is a detailed model of a jet engine, likely from a commercial or military aircraft. The central hub is surrounded by a series of evenly spaced, curved fan blades that radiate outward, forming a circular intake. The blades are metallic and have a dark, slightly reflective finish, with some subtle wear and tear visible on the edges. The intake cone at the center features a spiral marking, a common visual cue for jet engines. The cylindrical housing around the fan is smooth and metallic, with a slight taper toward the rear. The overall appearance suggests a modern turbofan engine, designed for high efficiency and thrust.
description: A metallic jet engine with curved fan blades and a spiral-marked intake cone, housed in a smooth cylindrical casing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spiral-marked intake cone and the full array of curved fan blades.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the engine and the profile of the fan blades within the cylindrical housing.
description_view_2: Rear-angled view focusing on the fan blade arrangement and the central intake hub.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the engine's cylindrical shape and the curvature of the blades.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Not applicable; only the engine is present, no wings visible.
attribute_tail_design: Not applicable; tail section not present or visible.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not applicable; landing gear not present or visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Not applicable; cockpit not present or visible.
